  • The settlement rule gives you the flexibility to capitalise a portion of maintenance costs to the existing fixed asset while expensing the remainder to a cost center. It is also possible to expense 100% of the maintenance costs. This can be done in a single settlement run.
  • If interim AuC-based balance sheet capitalisation is not required, an investment project without an investment profile simplifies the setup while retaining all cost-collection and project-stock capabilities.

7. Further Reading

  • SAP Community Blog: Settlement of Investment Projects in SAP Cloud ERP
  • SAP Help Portal: Scope Item 35F — Project Control: Capital Projects (Best Practices)

 

8. System Example Showing the Process Flow

1. Create Capital Investment Project
when creating the project, you need to use the Project Profile: Investment Project,
otherwise the functionality described in this blog will not be available

The assignment of an investment profile is optional.

  • With an investment profile assigned the system will automatically, with release of the project, create an asset under construction (AuC), you will then periodically or event-based settle the costs from the project to the AuC (move costs out of P&L and capitalise them in the balance sheet).
  • If no investment profile is assigned the system will not create an asset under construction. All the costs posted to the investment project can later be settled to a fixed asset, a cost center, another project,…

2. Capture Planned Costs and/ or Project Budget
If needed, you can upload planned costs or budgets to the investment project.
You can use the app Import Financial Plan Data (F1711) to upload planned costs or project budget.
Planned costs and budgets are differentiated via the plan category. Make sure you choose the category BUDGET01 for the budget upload.

3. Create Demands
If needed, demands can be planned for the investment project. Details are described in scope item 4RF- Project Control – Resourcing and Procurement

4. Purchase Materials into Project Stock

  • create a demand (as described in 3.), or
  • directly create a purchase order (app Manage Purchase Orders) with
    - account assignment category Q – Project – Make-to-Order
    - enter the WBS that should own the stock (item details), you do not need to enter a GL account
    The special stock indicator Q - Project Stock is derived automatically from the WBS account assignment.

    If the purchase order needs an approval, check the tab “Approval Details” to find the assigned approver.
    Approvals can be found via Self Services > My Inbox > Approve Purchase Order

5. Goods Receipt for Project Stock

  • Use the app Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document (F0843) to post a goods receipt into project stock.
  • Check your project reporting (Project Financial Booklet Booklet – Enterprise Projects) to see the balance sheet posting (material inventory) is assigned/ attributed to the investment project
    The project report shows the inventory account (BS) is account assigned to the project and therefore visible in project reporting. This is only done for project stock.
  • Check your material inventory to see the posted material quantity update in project stock (special stock indicator Q).
    This means, these materials are not freely available but can only be consumed by the assigned WBS element.
    app Stock – Multiple Materials (F1595)

6. Consume Material from Project Stock
Use the app Post Goods Movement (MIGO) to consume the material from project inventory to the investment project.
- Movement Type 221
- Special Stock Indicator Q
enter your WBS element as this project stock can only be consumed for the assigned WBS
the project report shows the consumption:

7. Post Costs to the Investment Project
e.g. activity allocation posting (app Manage Activity Allocation)


8. Maintain Settlement Rules
the settlement rule can be maintained in the project master (app Project Control – Enterprise Projects)

In this example 50% of the costs are capitalised and therefore settled to a fixed asset, the other 50% are settled to a maintenance cost center.
It would also be possible to settle 100% to cost center or other cost objects. There is no need to settle to a fixed asset.

9. Project Settlement
app Run Settlement – Actual

entry.title: Extending SAP Joule with SharePoint Grounding and MCP
entry.content:
What You Will Learn

In this blog post you will learn how to:

  • Ground SAP Joule in SharePoint documents using SAP AI Core Document Grounding so that Joule can answer questions directly from your document library
  • Build a chunking and embedding pipeline in SAP AI Launchpad that keeps the vector index fresh as documents change
  • Create custom Joule skills and agents in SAP Build that chain document search, structured extraction, and S/4HANA API calls into a fully automated workflow — no copy-paste, no form-filling
  • Connect a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server as a Joule toolkit so that warehouse operations can evolve independently of the agent definition

By the end of this two-part series, a warehouse operator can type "Process delivery note 54321" in Joule and have an inbound delivery created in S/4HANA automatically.

Prerequisites

Before you start, make sure you have:

  • An SAP BTP subaccount with entitlements for SAP AI Core (Extended plan) and SAP Build Process Automation
  • An SAP AI Launchpad instance connected to your AI Core service
  • An SAP S/4HANA Cloud tenant with the Inbound Delivery A2X API enabled, or access to SAP API Business Hub sandbox
  • A Microsoft 365 tenant with a SharePoint site containing your delivery note documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, or PPTX)
  • Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator access in Microsoft Entra ID — or a contact who has it — to grant admin consent for API permissions
  • Basic familiarity with SAP Build (Joule Studio) and BTP destinations

Introduction

SAP Joule is SAP's generative AI copilot embedded across the SAP ecosystem. It understands SAP transactional data well out of the box — but enterprise knowledge often lives outside SAP. Delivery notes, vendor contracts, material specs, and SOPs typically sit in SharePoint, Teams, or network drives. Joule cannot act on what it cannot read.

This walkthrough shows how we connected those two worlds. The result is a Joule Capability Package called Custom Agent Ideation that imports three reusable building-block packages and adds two orchestration agents and four Joule skills on top:

  • searchDeliveryNotes — instant vector search over SharePoint PDFs directly from Joule
  • deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery — end-to-end automation: search → extract → map → create inbound delivery in S/4HANA
  • createInboundDelivery — direct inbound delivery creation when you already have the data
  • warehouseTaskCreator — MCP-powered agent that creates warehouse tasks from an inbound delivery number

Architecture Overview

Joule Extensibility — Reference Architecture

The SAP reference architecture for Integrating and Extending Joule defines the canonical pattern that underpins everything in this walkthrough. At its core, Joule sits in the middle — connected to SAP BTP for extensibility, SAP Cloud Identity Services for authentication, and the broader SAP application estate (S/4HANA Cloud, SuccessFactors, Ariba, Concur) through a unified integration layer.

extend joule with joule studio

Source: SAP Architecture Center — Integrating and Extending Joule (Ref-Arch 464deb)

Joule's extensibility operates across four capability patterns:

Pattern Description

TransactionalNatural language triggers direct backend processes — purchase orders, approvals, document creation
NavigationalGuides users to the right application or screen without manual navigation
InformationalDelivers knowledge from SAP Help, customer documents, or indexed content via RAG
AnalyticalSurfaces business insights and performance metrics on demand

This walkthrough implements the Informational pattern (SharePoint document grounding) and the Transactional pattern (automated inbound delivery creation and warehouse task management) — showing how both can work together in a single Joule Capability Package built with Joule Studio in SAP Build.

This Demo — Solution Architecture

The diagram below shows how the components in this walkthrough connect at runtime. The Joule Capability Package (Custom Agent Ideation) sits on top of three reusable building-block packages and exposes four skills that Joule routes to based on the user's intent.

Imported Package Version Purpose

SharePoint Document Grounding Search1.1.0AI Core vector search over SharePoint
Delivery Note Assistant1.1.0SBPA action to retrieve and extract delivery note details
Inbound Delivery (A2X)1.1.3SBPA action to create inbound deliveries in S/4HANA

These three packages are SAP-maintained building blocks. You write only the orchestration logic that is unique to your scenario.

Custom Agent Ideation project overview showing imported package dependencies

Part 1: SharePoint Integration

Step 1 — Set Up SharePoint Grounding

This step produces the four credentials that AI Core needs to pull documents from SharePoint. You will set up a SharePoint site, register an Azure app, grant the right permissions, and validate access before wiring anything into AI Core.

1a — Prepare the SharePoint Site

  1. Sign in to sharepoint.com and create a new Communication site (recommended for document repositories).
  2. Upload your delivery note files. Supported formats include PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and images.
  3. Note the site URL: https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site-name> — you will need it in Step 2.

1b — Register an Azure App in Microsoft Entra ID

  1. Go to Azure Portal → Entra ID → App registrations → New registration.
  2. Name the app (e.g., SAP-DocumentGrounding) and complete the registration.
  3. From the Overview page, copy the Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID.
  4. Go to Certificates & Secrets → Client Secrets → New client secret.
    • Copy the Value immediately — it is shown only once. The Secret ID (GUID) is not the secret value.
  5. Go to API Permissions → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Application permissions.
    • Add Sites.Selected (Application type — "Access selected site collections").
    • Do not use Sites.Read.All — Sites.Selected is the least-privilege option and the correct permission for Document Grounding.
    • Click Grant admin consent. The Sites.Selected permission must show a ✅ status before you continue.
Caution: Admin consent requires a Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator. If you do not have that role, you cannot approve this yourself.
  • SAP employees: raise a request with your SharePoint / Azure Entra ID admin team to grant consent on the app registration.
  • External partners / customers: work with your organisation's SharePoint administrator to click Grant admin consent on your behalf.
Without this step, all API calls will return 403 Forbidden even if the permissions appear in the list.

Azure App Registration overview page showing Application and Directory (tenant) IDsAzure API permissions showing Sites.Selected granted with admin consent

1c — Grant the App Access to the SharePoint Site

Admin consent in Entra ID grants API-level access, but you also need to grant site-level read permission using PnP PowerShell:

Install-Module PnP.PowerShell

Connect-PnPOnline -Url "https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site-name>" -Interactive

Grant-PnPAzureADAppSitePermission `
  -AppId "<CLIENT_ID>" `
  -DisplayName "SAP Document Grounding" `
  -Site "https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site-name>" `
  -Permissions Read

1d — Validate Access (Optional but Recommended)

Get an access token and confirm the Graph API can reach your site before wiring it into AI Core:

# Get access token
curl -X POST "https://login.microsoftonline.com/<TENANT_ID>/oauth2/v2.0/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials&client_id=<CLIENT_ID>&client_secret=<CLIENT_SECRET>&scope=https://graph.microsoft.com/.default"

# Validate site access
curl -X GET "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/sites/<SHAREPOINT_DOMAIN>:/sites/<SITE_NAME>" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <ACCESS_TOKEN>"

A 200 response with site metadata confirms everything is wired correctly.

At the end of this step you should have four values ready:

Value Where to find it

Tenant IDEntra ID → App registration → Overview
Client IDEntra ID → App registration → Overview
Client SecretEntra ID → Certificates & Secrets → Value column
SharePoint Site URLSharePoint Admin Center or browser address bar

These four values feed directly into the AI Core secret in the next step.

Part 2: AI Core Service Integration

Step 2 — Create the SharePoint Grounding Generic Secret in AI Launchpad

AI Core needs your SharePoint credentials to pull documents during indexing. Store them as a Generic Secret in SAP AI Launchpad so they are never hardcoded in pipeline configuration.

  1. Open SAP AI Launchpad → AI Core Administration → Generic Secrets.
  2. Click Create and name the secret, e.g., sharepoint-grounding-secret.
  3. Paste in the credentials JSON:
{
  "tenantId": "<AZURE_TENANT_ID>",
  "clientId": "<CLIENT_ID>",
  "clientSecret": "<CLIENT_SECRET>",
  "sharepointSiteUrl": "https://<tenant>.sharepoint.com/sites/<site>"
}

AI Launchpad Generic Secret form showing Application (Client) ID fieldAI Launchpad Generic Secret form showing Client Secret field

  1. Save. This secret will be referenced by the chunking pipeline in Step 3.

Step 3 — Create the Document Chunking Pipeline

The chunking pipeline reads documents from SharePoint, splits them into semantically meaningful chunks, embeds them, and writes them to a vector collection in AI Core.

  1. In AI Launchpad, navigate to Pipelines → Create Pipeline.
  2. Select the Document Grounding template.
  3. Set the source to SharePoint and reference the secret from Step 2.
  4. Set chunk size and overlap. Recommended starting values: 512 tokens, 50-token overlap.
  5. Name the target collection — e.g., delivery-notes.
  6. Run the pipeline and wait for completion. Note the final document and chunk counts.
Tip: If you update documents in SharePoint regularly, schedule the pipeline to run on a recurring cadence so the vector index stays current.

Step 4 — Verify the AI Core Search Endpoint

Once the collection is populated, verify the search endpoint is working. This is what the searchDeliveryNotes skill will call at runtime.

  1. In AI Launchpad, go to Grounding → Collections and select delivery-notes.
  2. Note the Collection ID — you will use this as the id in the grounding filter in Part 2, Step 5.
  3. Set the retrieval config: Top K = 5, Similarity threshold = 0.7.
  4. Run a test query — e.g., "delivery note vendor material PO number" — and confirm chunks return correctly.

The SharePoint Document Grounding Search package imported in the project wraps this endpoint as a callable Joule function.

 

Part 3: SAP Build

Step 5 — Build the Actions

Actions in SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) wrap external API calls as reusable steps. The project imports two action packages.

5a — SharePoint Delivery Note Search (from Delivery Note Assistant package)

This action calls the AI Core Orchestration endpoint and returns the delivery note content as structured JSON. Under the hood it combines document grounding with an LLM extraction step in a single request.

  • Action identifier: post_getDeliveryNoteDetails
  • Destination: mocked_JOULEDELIVERYNOTEDEST (points to your AI Core endpoint)
  • Input: query (string) — the search phrase passed by the agent
  • Output: deliverycontentdetails — the extracted delivery note fields as a JSON string

The SBPA function is called asynchronously via the Joule skill integration gateway and uses long-polling (14 s timeout) to wait for the AI Core response.

The full request body sent to AI Core:

{
  "orchestration_config": {
    "module_configurations": {
      "llm_module_config": {
        "model_name": "gpt-4o",
        "model_params": { "max_tokens": 1000, "temperature": 0 }
      },
      "grounding_module_config": {
        "type": "document_grounding_service",
        "config": {
          "input_params": ["query"],
          "output_param": "groundingOutput",
          "filters": [
            { "id": "<YOUR_COLLECTION_ID>", "data_repository_type": "vector" }
          ]
        }
      },
      "templating_module_config": {
        "template": [
          {
            "role": "system",
            "content": "You are a delivery note extraction assistant. Extract all fields from the document context and return ONLY a valid JSON object with no markdown, no extra text, no code fences."
          },
          {
            "role": "user",
            "content": "Document context:\n{{?groundingOutput}}\n\nExtract all delivery note details and return as JSON."
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  },
  "input_params": { "query": "<natural language search query>" }
}

Key points to note:

  • filters.id — replace <YOUR_COLLECTION_ID> with the Collection ID from Step 4.
  • data_repository_type: "vector" — must be snake_case. Using dataRepositoryType (camelCase) returns a 400 error.
  • {{?groundingOutput}} — the AI Core Orchestration service injects retrieved document chunks here before calling the LLM.
  • The system prompt forces a clean JSON response with no markdown fences, because the downstream agent parses the content string directly.

5b — Create Inbound Delivery (from Inbound Delivery (A2X) package)

This action calls the S/4HANA A2X Inbound Delivery API to create the delivery record.

  • Action identifier: post_A_InbDeliveryHeader
  • Destination: mocked_inbound_delivery (points to your S/4HANA or API Hub endpoint)

Field Source in agent S/4HANA field

supplierdeliverydocumentDeliveryNoteNoDeliveryDocumentBySupplier
deliverydateDeliveryDate (ISO format)DeliveryDate
actualdeliveryquantityDeliveryQtyActualDeliveryQuantity
purchaseorderPONumberReferenceSDDocument
purchaseorderitemLineNoReferenceSDDocumentItem
batchnumberBatchNoBatch

Step 6 — Create the AI Agent Project in SAP Build

The project in SAP Build groups everything together. Create a new AI Agent Project and import the three dependency packages.

  1. In SAP Build, go to Lobby → Create → Build an Automated Process → AI Agent.
  2. Name it Custom Agent Ideation, set description to Customized Joule Agent for Public Cloud ERP.
  3. Import the three dependency packages:
    • SharePoint Document Grounding Search
    • Delivery Note Assistant
    • Inbound Delivery (A2X)
  4. Configure the system aliases under Settings:
    • SbpaService → destination: SAP_SBPA_API
    • APIAGENTCALLBACK → destination: AC_callback_api

These aliases let the function configuration in SAP Build refer to the SBPA integration gateway without hardcoding URLs.

Step 7 — Build the Delivery Note to Inbound Delivery Agent

This is the orchestration brain of the solution. The agent runs the full Search → Extract → Map → Create → Confirm flow automatically, without any user intervention.

Agent configuration:

agent: Delivery Note to Inbound Delivery Agent
base_model: gpt-5-minimal
iterations: 25
default_output_format: markdown

The automated process the agent follows:

  1. On any delivery note query, immediately call Search Delivery Notes with the query: "delivery note number, vendor, material, quantity, PO number, line item"
  2. Parse the JSON response and extract: DeliveryNoteNo, DeliveryDate, PONumber, LineNo, MaterialNo, DeliveryQty, BatchNo, Warehouse
  3. Map the extracted values to the inbound delivery API fields (see table in Step 5b)
  4. Call Create Inbound Delivery with the mapped parameters
  5. Reply to the user with a confirmation including the delivery note number, PO, quantity, and date

The agent instruction explicitly states: "The agent must never ask the user for any of these values or present options; the process must be strictly automated and sequential."

Content safety filtering is applied to all inputs (Azure Content Safety, threshold 4 for hate/self-harm/sexual/violence).

Expertise:

You are an expert in automating logistics workflows, specializing in the seamless extraction, mapping, and orchestration of delivery note data to inbound delivery processes without user intervention.

Instructions:

**Purpose & Objectives**
The Delivery Note to Inbound Delivery Agent assists users in fully automating the creation of inbound deliveries from delivery notes. Its objectives are:
- Automatically search for and extract delivery note information upon receiving relevant queries.
- Parse, map, and transfer all required delivery fields for inbound delivery creation, without requesting further input from the user.
- Ensure successful creation is promptly confirmed to the user.

**Process**
1. When the user requests a delivery note-related task (such as providing a delivery note number, vendor, material, PO, or line item):
   - Immediately call the 'Search Delivery Notes' tool with the query: "delivery note number, vendor, material, quantity, PO number, line item".
2. Upon receiving the JSON output from the tool:
   - Parse the JSON string and extract these fields:
     - DeliveryNoteNo
     - DeliveryDate
     - PONumber
     - LineNo
     - MaterialNo
     - DeliveryQty
     - BatchNo
     - Warehouse
   - Convert *DeliveryDate* with same format of '2026-04-27T00:00:00'
   - Map the extracted values as follows for the next step:
     - SupplierDeliveryDocument = DeliveryNoteNo
     - DeliveryDate = DeliveryDate
     - ActualDeliveryQuantity = DeliveryQty
     - PurchaseOrder = PONumber
     - PurchaseOrderItem = LineNo
     - BatchNumber = BatchNo
3. Using the mapped values, automatically invoke the 'Create Inbound Delivery' tool with the mapped input parameters.
4. Upon successful execution, confirm to the user what was created (including delivery note and PO details).
- The agent must never ask the user for any of these values or present options; the process must be strictly automated and sequential as above.

**Tooling**
- 'Search Delivery Notes': Retrieves delivery note and related document data based on query parameters.
- 'Create Inbound Delivery': Consumes extracted and mapped values to generate inbound deliveries.
- JSON Parsing: The agent must be capable of handling and extracting the required fields from complex JSON responses and mapping them precisely as outlined.
- The orchestration logic must guarantee a hands-free, predictable workflow for the user.

Additional Context:

**Communication**
- Maintain a professional, concise, and transactional tone; avoid any unnecessary small talk or ambiguity.
- Always state exactly what was done and which identifiers were involved.
- Never prompt the user for information or present choices about delivery note or inbound delivery details.

**Guidelines**
- If any required value is missing in the JSON output, clearly state which information was unavailable, but do not request user input to supply it.
- Follow the sequence of tools and logic strictly: Search → Extract → Map → Create → Confirm.
- Avoid speculation or assumptions—work strictly with the output you have.

**Examples**
**User:** "Please process this delivery note."
**Agent:** *[Searches delivery notes, extracts all required fields, creates inbound delivery, then replies:]*
"Inbound delivery created for Delivery Note #54321 (PO #98765, Qty: 100, Date: 2024-06-10)."

**User:** "Can you make an inbound delivery from this note?"
**Agent:** *[Performs end-to-end flow automatically; responds with confirmation of creation and details.]*

**Edge Case:**
**User:** "Process this delivery note."
**Agent:** *[If deliveryNoteNo is missing after parsing JSON:]*
"Unable to create inbound delivery: delivery note number was not found in the search results."

Step 8 — Create the Joule Skills (Scenarios)

Skills are what Joule sees — each scenario maps a user intent to a function, which in turn calls an agent or directly invokes an action. The project exposes four skills:

Skill (Scenario) Joule trigger description Target function

searchDeliveryNotesUser asks about delivery notes, vendors, materials, or shipmentssearchDeliveryNotes (direct action)
deliveryNotetoInboundDeliveryUser wants to convert a delivery note to an inbound deliverydeliveryNotetoInboundDelivery (agent-orchestrated)
createInboundDeliveryUser wants to create an inbound delivery directlycreateInboundDelivery (direct action)
warehouseTaskCreatorUser wants to create a warehouse taskwarehouseTaskCreator (agent-orchestrated via MCP)

Each agent-orchestrated skill works the same way at the function level: take the raw user message, pass it to the agent, and return the agent's response as markdown.

# deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery function (simplified)
- type: agent-request
  name: deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery
  body: <? agentRequestBody ?>
  result_variable: agentResponse

Step 9 — Build the Warehouse Task Creator Agent (MCP)

The Warehouse Task Creator is a second agent that handles a different part of the workflow: once an inbound delivery exists, create the corresponding warehouse task via the MCP server.

Agent configuration:

agent: Warehouse Task Creator
base_model: gpt-5-minimal

Process:

  1. Receive a user request containing an inbound delivery number
  2. Validate that the number is present and correctly formatted (numeric)
  3. Call the MCP warehouse task creation tool
  4. Return the task ID, or a clear error message if something goes wrong

MCP toolkit binding:

toolkits:
  - name: WarehouseTaskCreator
    type: mcp
    config:
      description: Create warehouse task based on inbound delivery document number.
      destination: warehouse-task-mcp
      namespace: warehouseTaskCreator.WarehouseTaskCreator0
      path: /mcp
      timeout: 60

The type: mcp is what distinguishes this agent from the previous one. Instead of calling an SBPA action group, it calls a Model Context Protocol endpoint at /mcp on the warehouse-task-mcp destination. Joule discovers available tools dynamically via the protocol — new warehouse operations can be added to the MCP server without touching the agent definition.

Expertise:

You are an expert in creating warehouse tasks in the MCP system, specializing in processing inbound delivery numbers and efficiently triggering warehouse operations based on user requests.

Instructions:

**Purpose & Objectives**
The Warehouse Task Creator assists users in automatically creating new warehouse tasks in the MCP system using the inbound delivery number provided in their prompt.
Success criteria include:
- Promptly creating a warehouse task whenever a valid inbound delivery number is given.
- Ensuring the user receives clear confirmation about task creation, including task details or error messages.

**Process**
1. Receive the user's request containing an inbound delivery number.
2. Validate that the inbound delivery number is present and correctly formatted.
3. When the number is valid, call the MCP warehouse task creation tool and submit the inbound delivery number as input.
4. Relay the response from the MCP tool back to the user, summarizing task details or clearly stating any encountered issues.
5. If input is missing or invalid, ask the user for clarification or a valid inbound delivery number.

**Tooling**
- MCP Warehouse Task Creation Tool: Use this tool to create a warehouse task by inputting the provided inbound delivery number. Always match the user's request to the required format before calling the tool.

Additional Context:

**Communication**
- Keep responses concise, straightforward, and informative.
- Avoid ambiguity—if the inbound delivery number is missing or incorrect, state the issue directly.

**Guidelines**
- Never guess or invent inbound delivery numbers; always ask the user to provide one if missing or unclear.
- Confirm task creation clearly and include key details or relevant error messages.

**Examples**
**User:** "Create a warehouse task for inbound delivery 5273849"
**Agent:** "A new warehouse task has been created for inbound delivery 5273849. Task ID: WT-00012345."
**User:** "Create a task for inbound delivery 123xyz"
**Agent:** "The inbound delivery number '123xyz' is invalid. Please provide a valid numeric inbound delivery number."

Step 10 — Configure the MCP Destination

The MCP server exposes warehouse operations as MCP-compliant tool definitions. The BTP destination is what connects the SAP Build agent to your deployed server.

  1. Deploy the MCP server to BTP (Cloud Foundry or Kyma). The tool namespace is warehouseTaskCreator.WarehouseTaskCreator0.
  2. In BTP Cockpit, create a destination named warehouse-task-mcp pointing to your deployed MCP service URL.
  3. Configure authentication (Bearer token recommended).
  4. In SAP Build, the warehouseTaskCreator agent picks up this destination automatically via the type: mcp toolkit binding.
  5. Test end-to-end by triggering the warehouseTaskCreator skill in Joule with a real inbound delivery number.

End-to-End Walkthrough

With everything deployed, here is what a warehouse operator experiences in Joule:

Scenario A — Search for a delivery note:

"Show me delivery note from Vendor ABC for PO 4500012345"

Joule routes to searchDeliveryNotes → SBPA calls AI Core Document Grounding Search → returns chunks from the SharePoint PDF → Joule displays the delivery note details.

Scenario B — Fully automated inbound delivery creation:

"Process delivery note 54321"

Joule routes to deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery → agent searches SharePoint → extracts and maps all fields automatically → calls S/4HANA A2X API → confirms:

"Inbound delivery created for Delivery Note #54321 (PO #98765, Qty: 100, Date: 2026-04-27)."

Scenario C — Warehouse task creation:

"Create a warehouse task for inbound delivery 5273849"

Joule routes to warehouseTaskCreator → agent validates the number → calls MCP server → confirms:

"A new warehouse task has been created for inbound delivery 5273849. Task ID: WT-00012345."

Summary

Step Component What Was Built

1Azure / SharePointApp Registration with Sites.Selected for least-privilege grounding access
2AI LaunchpadGeneric Secret storing SharePoint credentials
3AI Core PipelineDocument chunking and embedding pipeline → delivery-notes vector collection
4AI Core SearchVector search endpoint verified and ready
5SAP Build Actionspost_getDeliveryNoteDetails (SharePoint) + post_A_InbDeliveryHeader (S/4HANA A2X)
6SAP Build ProjectCustom Agent Ideation with three imported packages
7Joule AgentdeliveryNotetoInboundDelivery — automated Search → Extract → Map → Create
8Joule SkillsFour scenarios: search, end-to-end automation, direct create, warehouse task
9Warehouse AgentwarehouseTaskCreator — MCP-powered agent for warehouse operations
10MCPwarehouse-task-mcp destination + deployed MCP server

Key Design Decisions

Why two separate agents instead of one?

The delivery note flow is deterministic and fully automated — it always follows Search → Create with no branching. The warehouse task flow is simpler and uses a completely different backend (MCP vs. SBPA). Keeping them separate means each agent has a tight, auditable task definition and an independent iteration budget.

Why MCP for warehouse tasks?

MCP lets the warehouse operations backend evolve independently. New tools — cycle counting, pick confirmation, stock queries — can be added to the MCP server without touching the Joule agent definition. Joule discovers new tools dynamically via the protocol.

Why import packages instead of building from scratch?

The three imported packages (SharePoint Document Grounding Search, Delivery Note Assistant, Inbound Delivery (A2X)) are SAP-maintained building blocks. Using them gives you tested action definitions and upgrade paths for free. You write only the orchestration logic that is unique to your scenario.

Next Steps

  • Extend the document scope — add more SharePoint libraries (purchase orders, goods receipts) by adding source configurations to the chunking pipeline.
  • Chain the scenarios end-to-end — route deliveryNotetoInboundDelivery output directly into warehouseTaskCreator so that a single Joule prompt covers the entire inbound process.
  • Add locale formatting — the project already includes a localize function that formats dates and numbers according to the user's regional settings before displaying results.

Related Links

  • SAP Architecture Center — Integrating and Extending Joule
  • SAP AI Core Documentation — Document Grounding
  • SAP Build Process Automation — Joule Studio
  • SAP Joule Extensibility Overview
  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) — Official Specification
  • Microsoft Graph API — Sites.Selected Permission
  • PnP PowerShell — Grant-PnPAzureADAppSitePermission

Have questions or feedback? Drop a comment below — happy to help. You can also find me and my teammates (liling.jia@sap.com, xiaoming.chai@sap.com) on the SAP Community profile page.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition CE2608
entry.content:
Introduction

Every SAP S/4HANA Cloud release brings exciting innovations and continuous business improvements. While these enhancements deliver greater value to customers, they also introduce the need to validate the business processes continue to function seamlessly after every SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud release cycles (6-month release upgrade or monthly update).

Testing therefore becomes an essential part of every upgrade/update cycle. However, executing the complete regression suite for every upgrade/update is both time-consuming and resource-intensive. The key challenge is no longer whether to test but what to test and with what data and infer the tests with the right status.

As we continue to innovate and enhance the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool (TAT), the CE2608 release brings in further improvements aimed at simplifying test scoping, test data management, test status comparison of different Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) executions

In this blog, I’ll walk you through the following latest features delivered with CE2608.

  • AI-Powered Test Scoping in Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS)
  • Multiple Data Variant capabilities for Test Data
  • Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) Comparator enhancements

1)    AI-Powered Test Scoping in Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS)

To address the challenge of deriving the right scope automatically, Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) was introduced to automatically derive an optimized PUT scope based on SAP Standard code changes delivered in every major upgrade or monthly update.

While code-change analysis marked a major step towards intelligent regression testing, in this release we have introduced an additional optimization measure with AI-based scoping complementing the code-change-based approach.

Several additional factors like below are now considered whether a business process should be executed after an upgrade where AI has the potential to identify patterns that traditional rule-based approaches cannot

  • Customer Relevancy: Application usage information indicating how frequently customers execute individual business processes.
  • Process Complexity: The number of ABAP development objects touched by each automated test process across different object types
  • Code Change Object Ratio: The relationship between changed development objects and the total objects touched by an automation process
  • Historical Behaviour: Regression behaviour observed across previous releases

What's New in CE 2608

Based on the above factors, the AI model was trained and validated with simulated upgrade scenarios of previous releases to identify the right scope. Beginning with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud CE2608 release, Intelligent Test Scoper proposes an optimized regression scope using AI taking the below factors into consideration.

  • SAP Standard code changes
  • Customer Usage
  • Process complexity
  • Change-to-touch ratio
  • Historical learning from previous cloud releases

The result is a smarter regression scope that enables customers to:

  • Execute fewer but relevant regression tests without compromising on quality
  • Reduce upgrade validation effort
  • Shorten regression execution time
  • Lower testing costs

Unless the customer wants to change the scope in the Manage Upgrade Test app, there is no additional action required by the customer. By default, AI-based i-TS scope will be executed as part of Post Upgrade Tests.

2)    Simplifying Test Data Management with Creation of Multiple Data Variants and Assigning them to TDC Variants

Managing test data efficiently is critical for executing reliable business process tests. As organizations expand their test coverage across different business scenarios, creating and maintaining multiple test data variants can become repetitive and time-consuming.

To address this challenge, we have introduced a powerful enhancement to Test Data Container (TDC) that significantly simplifies test data management. With the introduction of a new entity called Dataset which brings the ability to create multiple data variants and assign them to TDC variants in a simple workflow, testers can prepare their test plans faster, reduce manual effort, and improve consistency across test executions.

The Challenge

Traditionally, when a test plan required multiple business scenarios, users had to:

  • Create individual data variants one at a time.
  • Assign TDC variants separately for each variant.
  • Repeat the same steps across multiple test scenarios.

While manageable for smaller test plans, this process became increasingly tedious and error-prone for enterprise-scale testing where dozens of variants may be required.

Introducing Dataset – A Smarter Way to Organize Test Data

The new Dataset entity brings a structured approach to organizing test data and easy consumption during data variant creation. A Dataset groups together multiple Test Data Container (TDC) variants into a single reusable entity.

Creating a Dataset

Creating a dataset is straightforward:

  1. Navigate to More Actions in the TDC application.
  2. Select Manage Dataset.
  3. Choose Create.
  4. Provide the dataset name, tag, and description.
  5. Select two or more Test Data Containers to include.
  6. Review the selections and confirm.

Once created, the dataset becomes available for future test plan executions.

Create Multiple Data Variants in One Go

Create multiple data variants and assign their corresponding test data with just a few clicks.

Within the Test Your Processes application:

  1. Open the required test plan.
  2. Navigate to the Variants tab.
  3. Select Create Data Variant and Assign TDC Variant

The application intelligently adapts based on the number of TDCs associated with the test plan.

Scenario 1: Single Test Data Container

When only one TDC is available:

  • Select one or more data variants.
  • Optionally modify the variant name and description.
  • Map each data variant to the appropriate TDC variant.
  • Select Create and Assign.

 

  • All selected variants are created and assigned in one operation.

     

Scenario 2: Multiple Test Data Containers

When multiple TDCs are part of the test plan:

  • Select from the available datasets
  • Review the corresponding descriptions.
  • Complete the mapping between data variants and datasets.
  • Select Create and Assign 
     
  • The system automatically assigns the appropriate TDC variants contained within the selected dataset, eliminating repetitive manual assignments. 

Key Benefits

This enhancement has significant advantages for customer testing teams:

  • Reduced Manual Effort – Create and assign multiple data variants in a single workflow.
  • Improved Productivity – Spend less time managing test data variants and more time validating business processes.
  • Better Organization—Related TDC variants are logically grouped for easier management.

 3)    Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) Comparator enhancements

The Post-Upgrade Test (PUT) Comparator enables users to compare any two completed executions of the same execution variant, either within the same release or across different releases. By comparing execution analytics and matching scope details side by side, users can quickly identify execution differences, analyze failures, and validate upgrade readiness.
With CE2608, several enhancements have been introduced to make comparison analysis more efficient and intuitive. 

Data Visibility & Pagination
Comparison results have been enhanced to provide better visibility into execution details while supporting pagination for large comparison datasets.
As organizations execute larger post-upgrade test suites, comparison results can contain hundreds of matching scope items. Pagination allows users to efficiently navigate through these datasets while maintaining a responsive and organized comparison experience.

Key Highlights
a. Improved visibility of execution comparison results.
b. Pagination support for large comparison datasets.
c. Faster navigation through matching scope items.

 Filtering & Usability
The PUT Comparator now includes enhanced filtering capabilities that help users quickly focus on the comparison results that matter most.
Instead of manually reviewing every matching scope item, users can filter comparison results based on execution status and other relevant criteria to rapidly identify failed, successful, or impacted processes. Combined with usability improvements across the interface, these enhancements make post-upgrade analysis faster and more efficient.

Key Highlights
a. Advanced filtering options for comparison results.
b. Quickly identify failed or impacted scope items.
c. Improved navigation and overall usability.
d. Faster analysis of execution differences across releases.

 

Additional Info

Links to other important blogs:

  • How to get started with the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Beginner’s Guide to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Test Automation for Functional Consultants and Testers
  • The video playlist for Test Automation Tool can be accessed here
  • Integrating SAP Cloud ALM and the Test Automation Tool [TAT] for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud

Please bookmark the test automation community page to keep up with the latest updates happening in the test automation space.

For more details on the functionality, please refer to the links below:

  • Post-Upgrade Tests App
  • Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) 
  • Manage Upgrade Tests 
  • Manage Your Test Processes
  • Test Your Processes
  • Test Data Container
  • Analyze Automated Test Results
  • Configure Conditional Authentication
  • Also refer FAQs

Questions, feedback, or tips? Comment below or connect with us on SAP Community! Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

entry.title: NR4 Canada Report in SAP DRC – Available for On-Premise and Private Edition, soon in Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

Canadian tax compliance just got a significant upgrade in SAP. We are excited to announce that the NR4 withholding tax report for Canada is now available in SAP S/4HANA 2023 and SAP S/4HANA 2025, released via SAP Note 3748353. In addition, it is planned to be delivered in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS02 (October 2026) and in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608.2 (October 2026).

The report is delivered as part of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) — the same solution that already hosts the T4A-NR report — giving Canadian customers a consistent, unified experience for non-resident withholding tax reporting.

This new report directly addresses a long-standing need expressed by our Canadian customers and complements the existing T4A-NR report that was already available in SAP.

What Is the NR4 Report?

The NR4 – Statement of Amounts Paid or Credited to Non-Residents of Canada is a form mandated by the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA). It must be filed by Canadian payers who have paid or credited amounts — such as dividends, royalties, rental income, management fees, or other passive income — to non-residents of Canada during a calendar year.

For every non-resident recipient, a corresponding NR4 slip must be issued, and an NR4 summary must be submitted to the CRA. This is a critical reporting obligation under the Income Tax Act of Canada and is distinct from the T4A-NR, which covers payments to non-residents for services rendered in Canada (such as acting or performance fees).

What's New: NR4 Report in SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

With the delivery of this new functionality, SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud now supports the automated generation of NR4 slips and summary directly from the withholding tax postings recorded in the system.

Key capabilities of the new NR4 report include:

  • Extraction of withholding tax data posted against non-resident vendors and business partners
  • Income type classification according to CRA-defined NR4 income codes (e.g., dividends, royalties, rents, management fees)
  • Currency conversion handling for amounts paid in foreign currencies, in line with CRA requirements
  • Country of residence derivation from business partner master data
  • Tax treaty code mapping based on the non-resident's country of tax residency
  • Gross income and withholding tax amounts per recipient, per income type
  • Output in the format required for CRA electronic filing

How It Complements the T4A-NR

We have heard your feedback. Many of our Canadian customers highlighted that while the T4A-NR report covered payments to non-residents for services, there was no equivalent SAP-delivered solution for the NR4, which covers passive income payments to non-residents. The NR4 report fills this gap.

 

Feature

T4A-NR

NR4

Payment type

Services rendered in Canada

Passive income (dividends, royalties, rents, etc.)

Recipient

Non-residents providing services

Non-residents receiving passive income

CRA form

T4A-NR slip

NR4 slip

Available in SAP DRC

Yes

Now available

 

Configuration: What You Need to Set Up

At the Business Partner Level

A key prerequisite is the correct setup of the Business Partner master data. For the NR4 report to correctly identify and extract non-resident payees, the following must be maintained at the business partner level:

  • Country of residence / tax country: The country where the non-resident is tax-resident must be maintained. This drives both the reporting and the applicable treaty withholding tax rate.
  • Withholding tax data: The relevant withholding tax type and code for Canada NR4 must be assigned to the business partner, including the non-resident's tax identification number (e.g., the Social Insurance Number, Business Number, or foreign TIN as applicable).
  • NR4 income type code: The CRA income type code (e.g., code 09 for rental income, code 11 for royalties, code 13 for management fees, code 25 for dividends) must be configured and associated with the applicable withholding tax type/code combination.

For full configuration details at the business partner level, refer to the SAP Help Portal: Business Partner Configuration for NR4 – SAP Help Portal

In SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

The NR4 report is executed from within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, the same way you already run the T4A-NR report. It allows you to:

  1. Select the reporting year and company code(s)
  2. Filter by withholding tax type relevant to NR4
  3. Generate the output for review, correction, and submission to the CRA

For the full functional documentation of the report, refer to the SAP Help Portal: NR4 Withholding Tax Report for Canada – SAP Help Portal

 

Availability Summary

 

EditionReleaseAvailability
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2023Available – SAP Note 3748353
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2025Available – SAP Note 3748353
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2025 FPS02Planned – October 2026
SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition2608.2Planned – October 2026 

 

Getting Started

If you are on SAP S/4HANA 2023 or 2025 on-premise, implement SAP Note 3748353 to activate the NR4 report. Ensure your Basis team has applied the note and that your Finance/Tax team reviews the business partner configuration prerequisites before the next reporting cycle.

If you are on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the functionality will be available with the 2608.2 release in October 2026 — no manual note implementation required.

 

We Want Your Feedback

The NR4 report was developed in direct response to customer demand, and your input continues to shape what we build next. If you have questions, configuration challenges, or suggestions for future enhancements, we invite you to share your feedback through our dedicated Work Zone community:

Canada ERP Localization – Work Zone

We look forward to hearing from you.

entry.title: Assembly Subcontracting with Sales Order Stock (E) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 7DW
entry.content:
Business Case

ABC Scooter Company is executing a Customer Order of 100 Scooters with Matte Black Panel. The Body Panel is manufactured specifically for the specific customer order (Sales Order Stock E), while Primer and Paint are standard consumables issued from Regular Plant Stock. The subcontractor paints the body panel and returns the finished Painted Black Body Panel back into Sales Order Stock (E).

By using Sales Order Stock (E) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the company ensures that all materials and subcontracting activities are strictly tied to the individual customer order, enabling precise cost tracking, eliminating cross-consumption.

Other applicable industries:

  • Industrial Machinery (custom components outsourced for specialist machining),
  • Medical Devices (patient-specific items outsourced for sterile packaging),
  • Electronics (customer-specific assemblies outsourced for specialist coating),
  • Apparel & Fashion (sales-order fabric sent to CMT vendor for cut-make-trim).

What changed in 2608:

  • The Subcontracting PO can now contain BOM with Components both from Regular inventory stock and Special stock (Sales Order Stock: E) in the same order.
  •  No stock type conversion required: E transfers directly to F at the vendor.
  • Full MTO support: materials and costs tied strictly to the individual customer sales order no cross-consumption.
  • No financial document at Goods Issue: the FI/accounting posting occurs only at Goods Receipt.

For scope item 7DW, the stock type transitions are:

  • Sales Order Stock component (e.g. RM027): E (at producer)  →  F Stock (at vendor)  →  Consumed on GR
  • Regular Plant Stock component: O (at producer)  →  O at vendor  →  Consumed on GR
  • Finished product (e.g. SG035): Received back into Sales Order Stock (E) on GR

Important: No financial or accounting document is generated when components are issued to the subcontractor (Goods Issue). This is a stock-to-stock transfer only no consumption has occurred yet. The financial posting is triggered only at Goods Receipt.

  • Example Master Data: SG035(main material), Components: RM027(Sales Order Stock)
  • In the material Master of RM027: Go to MRP4 and set the individual/Collective requirement as 1(Individual)
  • Note: The Individual/Collective Requirement = 1 (Individual) setting on MRP4 is mandatory. Without it, MRP will not plan the component against the specific sales order it will be treated as pooled stock and the Sales Order Stock (E) link will be lost.

  • For SG035: In the MRP2 Tab: maintain the following.

  • Go to the tab MRP4 and set the individual/Collective requirement as 1(Individual)

Process Steps:

Step 1: Create Sales Order

  • Create a Sales Order in the App: Manage Sales Order(F1873).

  • Ensure the Storage Location, shipping point is determined rightly
  • Save the Sales Order document and note down the number.

Step 2: Ensure Components are Available

  • Before running MRP, ensure the required components are available in the correct stock type:
  • Note: Either procure the components via a standard purchase order or production order referencing the sales order (so they land in Sales Order Stock E), or ensure sufficient stock already exists in the respective stock type before planning. Do not start MRP planning until the component stock is confirmed available.

Step 3: Run MRP

  • Schedule MRP Run in the background in the App: Schedule MRP Run(F1339) or
  • Go to the App: Manage Material Coverage(F0251) and run the MRP directly
  • Select the Material and click on details and Run MRP directly here.  Post MRP Run, for the respective Sales Order, a Purchase Requisition should be created. Note down the Purchase Requisition number.

Step 4: Create Subcontracting Purchase Order

  • Go to the App: Process Purchase Requisitions(F1048).
  • Enter the Purchase Requisition or filter based on your criteria, select the Purchase Requisitions and Assign the Supplier, should be determined based on the Purchase InfoRecords.

  • Then click Create Purchase Order, the Purchase Order Simulation should be displayed.
  • In the Items Table, the Account Assignment = M (Sales Order) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) must be pre-determined. Note: Account Assignment M = Make-to-Order / Sales Order. This ties the subcontracting PO to the specific customer sales order, ensuring all costs are tracked at order level. Do not change this value.

  • Select the Item, in the Material Data tab, click on components.

  • The Special Stock should be set for RM027 as E

  • Go to Account Assignment tab: Sales Order information should be determined.

  • In the Confirmation Tab, select Inbound Delivery under the Confirmation Control.
  • Save the Purchase Order and note down the number.

Step 5: Post Goods Issue to Subcontractor

  • Go to the App Subcontracting Cockpit (F2948) and enter the Purchase Order and click Go, the components details should be displayed.

  • Select the components and click Expand all

  • Then select all the components and click Create Delivery.

  • Ensure all the details are determined, For example for RM027, the Special indicator is E, Sales Order and Its Item number should be displayed

  • Then click Create Delivery. An outbound delivery document should be created, note down the number.
  • Login as Inventory Manager and check the Stock in the App: Display Stock Overview(MMBE) before posting goods issue.
  • Enter the material name SG035, Plant: 1010, Storage location as 101B and click execute

  • Enter Material as RM027, Plant 1010 and Storage loc as 101C and execute

  • Click Detailed display, the stock associated to respective Sales Order should be displayed.

  • Login as Shipping Specialist, go theApp: Change Outbound Delivery(VL02N).
  • Enter the outbound delivery document, which was created in the previous step, go to picking tab and complete the picking process and save the document.
  • Click post goods issue
  • Go to The Document flow of the Outbound Delivery and click on the material document which was created, check the below:
  • For RM027: The Special stock indicator should be E at the Producer and F once moved under Subcontractor’s Stock

  •  Important: No financial/accounting document is generated at Goods Issue, this is a stock-to-stock transfer only. No consumption has occurred yet. The FI/accounting document is generated only at Goods Receipt (Step 8).
  • Financial Document is not generated from outbound delivery, because it's only transfer the material from E stock to Subcontracting stock, no consumption has occurred yet.

Step 6 :Verify Stock at Subcontractor

  • Login as Inventory Manager.
  • You can as well check the stocks of each material in the App: Display stock overview(MMBE). And see the stock details, how many are with subcontracting vendor and how many with Producer. For example:

 

Step 7: Receive Inbound Delivery from Subcontractor

  • Login as Receiving Specialist and Go to App: Change Inbound Delivery(VL32N) and enter the Inbound Delivery number and press ENTER
  • Select the Item and go to the tab: Stock Placement, Enter the Putaway Qty and click Save.

Step 8: Post Goods Receipt

 Note: The Goods Receipt is the trigger for the financial posting. The finished product SG035 is received into Sales Order Stock (E) and the subcontracting fee + material costs are posted against the customer sales order.

  • Go to the App: Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery(F2502) and enter the Inbound delivery number which was created in the previous steps.
  • Check the Special Stock type as E and Sales Order and its item number information are predetermined.

  • Select the Item and check the details. Check the component details are reflected rightly with respective special stock type for the component Material RM027

  • Post the Goods Receipt and note down the material document.

Step 9: Verify Material Document and Journal Entry

Doc Info tab → FI Documents → Journal Entry

  • Check the Material Document, following details should be available and Accounting documents should be generated.

  • Click Doc Info tab and click FI Documents

  • Check the Journal Entry:

Step 10: Create Supplier Invoice

  • Note: this is optional because we are focusing on the logistic process

Step 11: Final Stock Verification

  • Check the Stock in theApp: Display Stock Overview(MMBE)

Summary:

Scope item 7DW closes a critical gap in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by enabling full MTO subcontracting with Sales Order Stock (E). Make-to-Order manufacturers can now outsource operations while maintaining complete sales order integrity, preventing cross-consumption, and ensuring accurate order-level cost tracking.

Related Resources:

Assembly Subcontracting with Project Stock (Q) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Scope Item 6GD

Restrictions

entry.title: Assembly Subcontracting with Project Stock (Q) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Scope Item 6GD
entry.content:
Business Case: 

Scooter Manufacturer: Body Panel Painting 

ABC Scooter Company is executing a Government Fleet Project. The Body Panel is manufactured specifically for the project (Project Stock Q), while Primer and Paint are standard consumables issued from Regular Plant Stock. The subcontractor paints the body panel and returns the finished Painted Body Panel back into Project Stock (Q). 

By using Project Stock (Q) in S/4HANA Public Cloud, all materials and subcontracting costs are tied to the specific WBS element, ensuring strict project-level control, accurate budget tracking, and full traceability, critical for delivering complex projects on time and within budget. 

What changed in 2608:

  • The Subcontracting PO can now contain BOM with Components both from Regular inventory stock and Special stock (Project Stock: Q) in the same order.
  •  No stock type conversion required: Q transfers directly to R at the vendor.
  • Full ETO support: all costs tracked to the WBS element throughout GI, GR, and FI.
  • No financial document at Goods Issue: the FI/accounting posting occurs only at Goods Receipt.

For scope item 6GD, the stock type transitions are:

  • Project Stock component (e.g. RM0614): Q (at producer)  →  R – Subcontractor Project Stock (at vendor)  →  Consumed on GR
  • Regular Plant Stock component (e.g. RM0613): Blank ( ) (at producer)  →  O at vendor  →  Consumed on GR
  • Finished product (e.g. SG0612): Received back into Project Stock (Q) on GR
  • No financial or accounting document is generated when components are issued to the subcontractor (Goods Issue). This is a stock-to-stock transfer only — no consumption has occurred yet. The financial posting (cost to WBS element) is triggered only at Goods Receipt.

How to Set-up: 

Example Master Data: SG0612(main material), Components: RM0613 (regular or Plant Stock) and RM0614(Project Stock) 

  • In the material Master of RM0614: Go to MRP4 and set the individual/Collective requirement as 1(Individual) 
  • RM0613: Go to MRP4 and set the Individual/Collective Requirement as 2 (Collective) this allows the component to be planned pooled from plant stock.
  •  Note: The Individual/Collective Requirement setting on MRP4 is the most commonly missed configuration. RM0614 must be set to 1 (Individual) so MRP plans it per WBS element, not pooled. Without this, the project stock link will not be established correctly.

If the stock is insufficient, then Produce or Procure the components RM0613 and RM0614 according to your scenario. 

Step-by-Step Process Guide 

Step 1: Create Project Demand

  • Create a demand in the App: Manage Project Demand(F4562) by entering details as shown in the screenshot. 

Step 2: Run MRP

  • Either schedule MRP Run in the background or Run it as shown below: 
  • Go to the App: Monitor Material Coverage - Net and Individual Segments (Version 2)(F2101A)
  • Enter the Shortage definition as MRP Standard, Material as SG0612 (For which the demand is created in the above step) and then click Go. The details are listed. 

  • Select the demand and click Start MRP Run. 

  • After the MRP run, click Manage Materials. 

  • A Purchase Requisition should be created. 

Step 3: Create Subcontracting Purchase Order

  • Go to the App: Process Purchase Requisitions(F1048A).
  • Enter the Purchase Requisition or filter based on your criteria, select the Purchase Requisitions and assign the supplier, supplier should be determined based on the Purchase InfoRecords.
  • Then Click Create Purchase Order, The Purchase Order Simulation should be displayed.
  • Account Assignment = Q (Project/WBS) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) must be pre-determined in the PO item table. Do not change these values.
  • In the Item Table, ensure that the account Assignment(Q) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) pre-determined. Select the Item, in the Material Data tab, click on components. 

  • The Special Stock field must be set to Q for RM0614 whereas for RM0613 it should be blank 

  • Go to Account Assignment tab: WBS information should be determined. 

  • In the Confirmation Tab, select Inbound Delivery under the Confirmation Control. 
  • Save the Purchase Order and note down the number. 

Step 4: Post Goods Issue to Subcontractor

  • Go to the App:Subcontracting Cockpit (F2948) and enter the Purchase Order and click Go, the components details should be displayed. 

  • Select the components and click Expand all 

  • Then select all the components and click Create Delivery. 

  • Ensure all the details are determined, For example for RM0614, the Special indicator is Q, WBS information is as same as for which the demand was generated.  

  • Then click Create Delivery. An outbound delivery document should be created, note down the number. 
  • Login as Inventory Manager and check the Stock in the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185) before posting goods issue. 

  • Login as Shipping Specialist and go to theApp: Change Outbound Delivery(VL02N).
  • Enter the outbound delivery document, which was created in the previous step, go to picking tab and complete the picking process and save the document. 
  • Click Post Goods Issue 
  • Go to The Document flow of the Outbound Delivery and click on the material document which was created, check the below: for RM0613, once the stock moves to Subcontractor stock, it should be denoted with Special Stock type O (Subcontractor Stock). 

  • Whereas for RM0614: The Special stock indicator should be Q at the Producer and R once moved under subcontractor’s stock 

  •  Important: No financial/accounting document is generated at Goods Issue this is a stock-to-stock transfer only. No consumption has occurred yet. The FI/accounting document is generated only at Goods Receipt (Step 7) 

Step 5: Verify Stock at Subcontractor

  • Login as Inventory Manager and go to the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185) and check the stock details: 
  • You can as well check the stocks of each material in the App: Display stock overview(MMBE). And see the stock details, how many are with subcontracting vendor and how many with Producer. For example: 

Step 6 : Receive Inbound Delivery from Subcontractor

  • Login as Receiving Specialist and go to App: Change Inbound Delivery(VL32N) and enter the Inbound Delivery number and press ENTER
  • Select the Item and go to the tab: Stock Placement, Enter the Putaway Qty and click Save. 

Step 7: Post Goods Receipt

  • Note: The Goods Receipt is the trigger for the financial posting. The finished product SG0612 is received into Project Stock (Q) and the subcontracting fee + material costs are posted to the WBS element.
  • Go to the App:Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery(F2502) and enter the Inbound delivery number which was created in the previous steps. 
  • Check the Special Stock type as Q and WBS information are predetermined. 

  • Select the Item and check the details. 
  • Check the component details are reflected rightly with respective special stock type for the component Material RM0613 and RM0614. 

  • Post the Goods Receipt and note down the material document. 

Step 8: Verify Material Document and Journal Entry

Doc Info tab → FI Documents → Journal Entry

  • Check the Material Document, following details should be available and Accounting documents should be generated. 

  • Click Doc Info tab and click FI Documents 

  • Check the Journal Entry: 

 Step 9: Create Supplier Invoice

  • Note: this is optional because we are focusing on the logistic process

Step 10 : Final Stock Verification

  • Check the Stock in the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185)

Summary:

Scope item 6GD closes a critical gap in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by enabling full ETO subcontracting with Project Stock (Q). Manufacturers can now outsource operations while maintaining strict WBS-level cost control, complete material traceability, and automated financial posting all within a single integrated process.

Related Resources:

Assembly Subcontracting with Sales Order Stock (E) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 7DW

Restrictions

entry.title: AI Powered i-TS (Intelligent Test Scoper) for Post Upgrade Test of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Releases
entry.content:
Introduction

Every SAP S/4HANA Cloud release brings exciting innovations and continuous business improvements. While these enhancements deliver greater value to customers, they also introduce the need to validate if already available business processes continue to function seamlessly post upgrade to every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Continuous Feature delivery (CFD)

Testing therefore becomes an essential part of every upgrade cycle. However, executing the complete Test Suite for regression test of every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release and CFD is time and resource intensive. The key challenge is no longer whether to test, but what to test?.

This challenge inspired the development of the Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) (See Figure-1) an application designed to recommend the optimal Post Upgrade Test (PUT) scope for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers.

Figure-1

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud CE 2608 release, we introduce AI-powered i-TS that delivers more efficient yet effective scope for Post Upgrade Tests, thereby further reducing the TCO for out esteemed Customers.

The Journey of Intelligent Test Scoper

Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) are a key offering for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers to validate software upgrades before consumption in productive environments.

The objective is simple:

  • Maintain software quality
  • Reduce effort and upgrade validation time
  • Sustain confidence in Release quality

Traditionally, PUT testing relied on executing even the entire suite of standard scripts delivered (via SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool) . While this approach maximized confidence, it also resulted in significant execution effort and longer upgrade cycles, not to mention higher TCO.

To address this challenge, Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) was introduced to automatically derive an optimized PUT scope based on SAP Standard code changes delivered in every Cloud Release or Continuous Feature Delivery (CFD).

How i-TS Determines the Optimized Scope

The working principle of i-TS can be understood using the following Venn diagram.

Consider the rectangle representing all development objects within an application area.

  • The blue circle represents the objects touched by SAP Standard PUT automates.
  • The yellow circle represents the objects changed in a Cloud Release or CFD.

The overlapping region identifies the automation processes affected by code changes. These processes form the optimized regression scope proposed by Intelligent Test Scoper.

This approach has successfully enabled customers to execute only those automated tests that are technically impacted by a release, reducing unnecessary regression execution.

Why Go Beyond Code Change Analysis?

While code-change analysis marked a major step in the direction of intelligent regression testing, our engineering team continued asking an important question:

Can Artificial Intelligence further reduce the scope proposal while maintaining the same quality standards?

The answer lay in recognizing that code changes alone do not always represent the complete business impact. Several additional factors influence whether a business process should be executed after an upgrade:

  • How frequently customers use the application
  • The overall technical complexity of a test process
  • The relationship between changed and executed development objects
  • Historical regression behaviour observed across previous releases

By learning from these signals collectively, AI has the potential to identify patterns that traditional rule-based approaches cannot.

Building the AI Foundation

As with every successful AI initiative, the journey starts with data. Our objective was not to replace the existing code-change logic, but to enhance it with additional intelligence derived from historical observations.

The AI model was trained using multiple dimensions of information, including:

  • Code Change Information: The optimized scope proposed by the existing i-TS solution based on SAP Standard code changes.
  • Customer Relevancy: Application usage information indicating how frequently customers (cumulatively) execute individual business processes.
  • Process Complexity: The number of ABAP development objects touched by each automated test process across different object types.
  • Change-to-Touch Ratio: The relationship between changed development objects and the total objects touched by an automation process.

Together, these parameters provide a richer representation of the business impact associated with every automated test process.

Training the AI Model

Building confidence in AI recommendations required extensive validation; Instead of relying only on historical accuracy, we simulated real upgrade scenarios by training the model on previous releases and predicting scope for future releases.

 

Training ReleasePrediction Release
CE 2502CE 2408
CE 2502 + CE 2408CE 2508
CE 2502 + CE 2408 + CE 2508CE 2602

This approach closely mirrors the way customers consume SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrades in production. For every prediction cycle, the AI-generated scope was carefully validated against actual PUT outcomes to ensure there was no quality leakage while measuring optimization gains.

This iterative learning process enabled continuous refinement of the prediction model before introducing it into productive scope recommendations.

The Results

The outcome exceeded our expectations. Earlier releases of Intelligent Test Scoper relied exclusively on SAP Standard code-change analysis and consistently delivered approximately 10% optimization in comparison to the full suite of standard automates.

With of AI-powered scope optimization, i-TS is now able to achieve nearly double the optimization efficiency while continuing to maintain the same quality standards.

Rather than replacing engineering expertise, AI complements the existing code-change analysis by learning from historical release behaviour and customer usage patterns to make more informed scope recommendations.

What's New in CE 2608

Beginning with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud CE 2608 release, Intelligent Test Scoper proposes optimized regression scope using Artificial Intelligence.

The AI-powered scope recommendation combines:

  • SAP Standard code changes
  • Process complexity
  • Change-to-touch ratio
  • Historical learning from previous cloud releases

The result is a smarter regression scope that enables customers to:

  • Execute fewer regression tests without compromising quality
  • Reduce upgrade validation effort
  • Shorten regression execution time
  • Lower testing costs
  • Increase confidence during every SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrade

Looking Ahead

AI is transforming the way software quality engineering is approached, and Intelligent Test Scoper is an important milestone in that journey.

The introduction of AI-powered scope optimization in CE 2608 demonstrates how historical engineering knowledge, customer usage insights, and machine learning can work together to deliver more intelligent testing recommendations.

As we continue evolving i-TS, our focus remains unchanged: helping SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers perform faster, smarter, and more reliable upgrade testing while maximizing efficiency and maintaining the highest standards of software quality.

We are excited about this journey and look forward to continuously enhance the capabilities to Intelligent Test Scoper in future releases. Refer following Help Portal Documentation to learn more about i-TS (Intelligent test Scoper)

entry.title: Talk to Your Localized Reports: How Joule Runs A/P, A/R, and Stock Reports in Native Language
entry.content:
Running a country-specific aging report used to mean remembering the transaction code, the exact parameter names, and the local compliance rules that shape every field. Now you can just ask.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces AI-assisted localized business data management — a new capability that lets Joule, SAP's generative AI digital assistant, run classic localized reports through natural-language prompts while respecting every country- and region-specific requirement.

Scope Note

This blog post covers SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the new AI Case Manage Business Data Using Localized Classic Applications. Activation requires the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG and the corresponding business catalog per report. Three localized reports are currently in scope: the A/P Aging Report, the A/R Aging Report, and the Stock Overview Report. The reports were originally built for specific countries, but any customer can adopt them wherever they see a fit — simply copy the delivered business catalog into your own business role and assign it to the target users. Other editions (on-premise, Cloud Private Edition) and other localized reports are not covered here.

Main Insight

Localized reports are where compliance meets complexity. Every country encodes its own rules for aging buckets, currency handling, cleared-item treatment, and inventory posting — and each rule shows up as another parameter on a classic report screen.

The result: teams spend more time configuring the report than reading it. New users take weeks to get productive. And every parameter mis-entry is a potential compliance gap.

AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management flips that. Joule sits on top of the same classic reports your finance and supply-chain teams already use — but it understands the country-specific context. You describe what you need in plain English. Joule fills the parameters, respects the localization rules, and runs the report.

You keep the compliance-certified backend. You lose the friction.

Three Reports, One Conversational Interface

Three classic localized reports ship with the initial release. Each requires its own business catalog assigned to the user.

1. Accounts Payable (A/P) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AP_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, supplier, and key date · select item types · configure due-date buckets and currency · analyze amounts in transaction or reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/P Aging Report for specific suppliers or supplier ranges within a company."

2. Accounts Receivable (A/R) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AR_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAR_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, customer, and key date · include or exclude cleared and special G/L items · define due-date buckets and reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/R Aging Report for customers S13100251 to S13100253 in company 1310."

3. Stock Overview Report

  • Report ID: J_3RMOBVEDH
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_MOBVED_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by plant and product number · select a posting date or date range · filter by storage location and batch · review inventory status in context
  • Example prompt: "Run the Stock Overview Report for product tg-d010_ua in plant 6510 with posting date today."

Same reports. Same compliance guarantees. New way to reach them.

Not restricted to the original country. These reports ship as SAP-delivered business catalogs, but you don't have to run them only in the country they were originally designed for. Copy the delivered catalog into a custom business role and assign the role to any user or business unit that needs the same reporting logic. For step-by-step guidance, see Copying and Editing Business Roles — SAP Help Portal.

Activation in 6 Steps

The capability is off by default. Enable it with the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG:

  1. Open your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition configuration environment with an administrator user that has the required authorization.
  2. Search for and open the configuration activity Activate Features.
  3. In the feature list, search for the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG.
  4. Review the feature details to confirm you are activating the AI-assisted localized business data management capability.
  5. Choose the activation option and confirm when prompted.
  6. Wait until the system confirms successful activation, then assign the required business catalog(s) to your users.

Once activated, users can invoke Joule from any Joule-enabled entry point in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and prompt the localized reports directly.

Practical Takeaway

Start with one report and one country. Pick the localized report your team runs most often — A/P Aging for a China entity is a good candidate — and follow this path:

  • Verify your system runs SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Joule enabled
  • Activate feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG in a test tenant
  • Assign the matching business catalog (e.g. SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC) to a pilot user
  • Ask Joule to run the report using one of the example prompts above
  • Compare Joule's parameter selections to what your team would have entered manually
  • Roll out to the full team once you confirm the compliance rules and outputs match

You'll know it's working when your finance team stops asking "which parameter goes where?" and starts asking "can I get the same report for last month?" — because now they can.

Summary

Localized reports carry the weight of country-specific compliance, and that complexity usually lives in the report screen.
